mission.title: prometheus: Configurable limit for Prometheus's disk space usage
mission.description: Configurable limit for Prometheus's disk space usage. Requested by 16+ users.

steps.title: Check current Prometheus version and storage configuration
steps.description:
Verify you are running Prometheus 2.x or later, which supports size-based retention:
```bash
prometheus --version
# Or in Kubernetes:
kubectl exec -n prometheus deploy/prometheus-server -- prometheus --version
```
Check current storage flags:
```bash
ps aux | grep prometheus | grep storage
```

steps.title: Configure size-based retention with --storage.tsdb.retention.size
steps.description:
Add the `--storage.tsdb.retention.size` flag to limit disk usage. This can be combined with time-based retention:
```bash
prometheus \
  --storage.tsdb.retention.size=50GB \
  --storage.tsdb.retention.time=30d \
  --storage.tsdb.path=/var/lib/prometheus/data
```
Supported units: B, KB, MB, GB, TB, PB. When the limit is exceeded, Prometheus removes the oldest data blocks first.

steps.title: Apply in Kubernetes via Helm or manifest
steps.description:
If running in Kubernetes with the prometheus-community Helm chart:
```bash
helm upgrade prometheus prometheus-community/prometheus \
  --set server.retention=30d \
  --set server.retentionSize=50GB
```
Or add the flags directly to the container args in your Deployment manifest.

steps.title: Verify retention is working
steps.description:
Check that Prometheus is respecting the size limit:
```bash
# Check current TSDB stats via the API
curl -s http://localhost:9090/api/v1/status/tsdb | python3 -m json.tool
# Check disk usage
du -sh /var/lib/prometheus/data
```
Monitor the `prometheus_tsdb_size_retentions_total` metric to confirm size-based deletions are occurring when needed.

resolution.summary: Prometheus added the `--storage.tsdb.retention.size` flag (available since Prometheus 2.x) to limit disk space usage. When the configured size is exceeded, Prometheus removes the oldest data blocks. Use in combination with `--storage.tsdb.retention.time` for both time and size-based retention.
codeSnippets:
# Limit Prometheus disk usage to 50GB and retain data for 30 days
prometheus \
  --storage.tsdb.retention.size=50GB \
  --storage.tsdb.retention.time=30d \
  --storage.tsdb.path=/var/lib/prometheus/data

# Kubernetes Deployment example
containers:
- name: prometheus
  image: prom/prometheus:v2.45.0
  args:
    - '--config.file=/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml'
    - '--storage.tsdb.retention.size=50GB'
    - '--storage.tsdb.retention.time=30d'
    - '--storage.tsdb.path=/prometheus'

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with prometheus installed or the issue environment reproducible.
